recommendations: add a minimal but meaningful MCP smoke test suite (one test per exposed endpoint plus one end-to-end `load -> Phonopy -> write_phonopy_yaml` flow)
pin and align dependencies across root and `mcp_output/requirements.txt` (including optional scientific extras via extras groups) to improve reproducibility
harden CI by adding a dedicated MCP workflow matrix (Linux/macOS/Windows
supported Python versions) with import-mode and CLI-fallback checks
introduce contract validation for endpoint schemas using Pydantic models with strict typing and example payload fixtures
reduce API surface fragility by wrapping high-risk core calls (`api_phonopy.py`
`interface/calculator.py`) behind thin stable adapter methods and explicit error normalization
add structured logging and request/latency/error instrumentation in `mcp_service.py` with correlation IDs for easier debugging
document endpoint-to-phonopy mapping and version compatibility in `README_MCP.md` (including known optional dependency behavior like `symfc`/`scipy`)
add regression tests for file I/O round-trips (`FORCE_SETS`
YAML) using small golden files to catch format drift
enforce static quality gates (ruff/black/mypy or pyright) in pre-commit and CI for both source and MCP plugin code
create performance benchmarks for heavy paths (YAML load
mesh/band setup) and set alert thresholds to detect slowdowns early

success_factors: End-to-end workflow status is success with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Low-intrusion import-mode adapter strategy was appropriate for phonopy and aligned with high import feasibility (0.9).
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio, indicating viable runtime packaging and entrypoint wiring.
Repository already has mature modular APIs (api_phonopy, YAML/file I/O, calculator interfaces), which reduced integration friction.
failure_reasons: Quality gate gap: original project test run is marked not passed despite '100%' coverage metadata, indicating test execution metadata inconsistency or skipped baseline tests.
Observability gap: per-node timings, LLM/DeepWiki calls, and resource metrics are largely missing/zero, limiting diagnostic confidence.
DeepWiki analysis failed (non-blocking), reducing automated architecture/context enrichment.

node_performance.download_time: Completed within total 230.34s runtime; repo imported via zip fallback successfully (315 files). Exact node duration not provided.
node_performance.analysis_time: Analysis completed and identified core modules/endpoints; exact timing unavailable due to missing per-node telemetry.
node_performance.generation_time: Code artifacts generated successfully (7 files), but reported LOC/file-size metrics are zero, suggesting metrics collection issue.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health checks passed; original project test execution appears not actually performed or not recorded.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Undetermined from telemetry (reported 0 MB). Instrumentation required before firm judgment.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Undetermined from telemetry (reported 0%). Current data quality insufficient for efficiency claims.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low incremental footprint (few generated files), but generated size metric is zero so actual footprint is not measured.
